Responsibility: Represent autonomous stakeholders with mission planning, recommendation reception, action decision, execution, and memory
Main Inputs: Ecosystem state S(t), scenario state, recommendation Rec_i(t), stakeholder parameters
Main Outputs: Mission intensity M_i(t), accepted action A_i(t), executed workload, updated local state
Related MOEE Concept: Stakeholder autonomy; Mission Intensity Graph; RMOV
Interacts With: Ecosystem Container; PUDAL Controller; MSRS Engine; Metrics Engine
Streamlit / UI Role: Displayed as stakeholder configuration and simulation state
Reuse Potential: High; subclass for GRACE, water distribution, smart loan, healthcare, and other cases

Responsibility: Maintain Resource, Mission, Output, and Value accounts for each stakeholder
Main Inputs: Resource changes, mission intensity, produced outputs, value gains/losses
Main Outputs: Updated RMOV account state for each stakeholder
Interacts With: Stakeholder Agent Core; Metrics Engine; PSKVE Ledger
Streamlit / UI Role: Can be displayed as stakeholder account table
Reuse Potential: High; generic for all MOEE domains

Responsibility: Store and retrieve time-varying mission intensity values for stakeholders
Main Inputs: Planned mission intensity, accepted recommendations, time step t
Interacts With: Stakeholder Agent Core; PUDAL Controller; Metrics Engine
Streamlit / UI Role: Can generate mission intensity plots and synchronization charts

Responsibility: Record Product, Service, Knowledge, Value, and Environment exchanges among stakeholders
Main Outputs: PSKVE ledger table and exchange history
Interacts With: Ecosystem Container; Stakeholder Agents; Metrics Engine
Streamlit / UI Role: Can be displayed as exchange log or stakeholder network table
Reuse Potential: High; exchange categories remain stable across domains

Responsibility: Apply disturbances and scenario conditions to the ecosystem during simulation
Main Inputs: Scenario name, base state, time step t, scenario parameters
Main Outputs: Scenario-adjusted state variables such as demand multiplier, cost multiplier, supply multiplier, and capacity multiplier
Interacts With: Simulation Runner; Ecosystem Container; Metrics Engine
Streamlit / UI Role: Scenario selection widget and scenario comparison table
Reuse Potential: High; new domains can define additional scenario libraries

Responsibility: Generate multi-stakeholder recommendation vectors according to selected policy objectives
Main Inputs: Interpreted ecosystem state, policy name, stakeholder list, objective weights
Core Classes / Files: MSRSPolicy; BalancedEVIPolicy; CostFirstPolicy; NutritionFirstPolicy; WasteFirstPolicy; core/msrs.py
Interacts With: PUDAL Controller; Stakeholder Agents; Metrics Engine
Streamlit / UI Role: Policy selection widget and recommendation summary
Reuse Potential: High; policies can be replaced or extended for each domain

Responsibility: Coordinate Perception, Understanding, Decision, Action, and Learning stages
Main Inputs: Ecosystem state S(t), MSRS policy, previous outcomes, mission goals
Main Outputs: Recommendations, action dispatch, learning log, interpreted state
Interacts With: Ecosystem Container; MSRS Policy Engine; Stakeholder Agents; Metrics Engine
Streamlit / UI Role: Can show PUDAL log, recommendation flow, and decision trace
Reuse Potential: High; central coordination layer for all cases

Responsibility: Compute normalized performance metrics and Ecosystem Value Index
Main Inputs: Domain outcomes, demand, served output, waste, profit, satisfaction, nutrition, scenario state
Main Outputs: Fulfillment, waste reduction score, profitability score, sustainability, mission achievement, EVI
Streamlit / UI Role: Summary metrics, time-series plots, downloadable results
Reuse Potential: Medium-High; common metrics reusable, domain metrics extendable

Responsibility: Hold agents, ledger, controller, context, history, and the step-by-step simulation loop
Main Inputs: Agents, scenario-adjusted state, model type, controller, domain case configuration
Main Outputs: Simulation history rows, domain outcomes, updated stakeholder states
Streamlit / UI Role: Provides backend state for dashboard and experiments
Reuse Potential: High; inherited by all domain cases

Responsibility: Execute one or more simulation runs across models, scenarios, policies, and time horizons
Main Inputs: Ecosystem factory, model, scenario, policy, number of time steps
Main Outputs: Simulation result dataframe, multi-run experiment results
Interacts With: Scenario Engine; Ecosystem Container; Metrics Engine
Streamlit / UI Role: Runs simulations when user clicks Run

Responsibility: Instantiate the campus food ecosystem with students, vendors, suppliers, operator, university, and regulator
Main Inputs: Campus food configuration, model type, policy name
Interacts With: Ecosystem Container; Campus Food Agents; PUDAL Controller
Streamlit / UI Role: Default case loaded by the dashboard
Reuse Potential: Medium; specific to campus food, but serves as template for other cases

Responsibility: Define domain-specific stakeholder behavior for students, vendors, suppliers, university, operator, and regulator
Main Inputs: Campus demand, capacity, costs, price, nutrition score, recommendation, scenario state
Main Outputs: Meal demand, production quantity, supply capacity, policy action, regulatory action, stakeholder outputs
Core Classes / Files: StudentAgent; VendorAgent; SupplierAgent; UniversityAgent; OperatorAgent; RegulatorAgent; cases/campus_food/agents.py
Interacts With: Campus Food Case Builder; Ecosystem Container; PUDAL Controller
Streamlit / UI Role: Provides stakeholder behavior behind simulation results
Reuse Potential: Low-Medium; behavior is domain-specific but subclassing pattern is reusable

Responsibility: Store default case parameters such as vendor capacity, nutrition score, unit cost, price, and stakeholder counts
Main Inputs: Manual parameter values or future YAML/JSON configuration
Interacts With: Campus Food Case Builder; Simulation Runner; Dashboard
Reuse Potential: Medium-High; should become generic YAML/JSON configuration in later versions

Responsibility: Provide interactive user interface for model, scenario, policy, and time-horizon selection
Main Inputs: User selections, simulation parameters, generated result tables
Main Outputs: Summary table, time-series plots, detailed result table, downloadable CSV
Interacts With: Simulation Runner; Metrics Engine; Campus Food Case Builder
Reuse Potential: Medium; can evolve into generic app with selectable cases

Responsibility: Run simulations from terminal for reproducible experiments and batch execution
Main Inputs: CLI arguments: model, scenario, policy, days, output path
Main Outputs: Printed summary and optional CSV file
Interacts With: Simulation Runner; Campus Food Case Builder
Streamlit / UI Role: Not used directly; supports non-interactive experiments

Responsibility: Save detailed simulation results for paper tables, figures, and external analysis
Main Inputs: Simulation dataframe and selected output path
Main Outputs: CSV files for results and summaries
Core Classes / Files: CSV export in Simulation Runner, CLI runner, and Streamlit app

Reuse Potential: High; applicable to all cases and experiments

Responsibility: Provide smoke tests to verify that the simulation runs and produces key outputs
Interacts With: Simulation Runner; Campus Food Case Builder
Streamlit / UI Role: Not used directly; supports quality assurance
Reuse Potential: High; should expand for every new case

Responsibility: Explain architecture, roadmap, and steps for creating new MOEE cases
Main Inputs: Framework design, example implementation, extension notes
Streamlit / UI Role: Not used directly; can be linked from future dashboard
Reuse Potential: High; essential for scaling to GRACE, water, smart loan, and other ecosystems
